title: quercus × subglaucescens
intro: Quercus × subglaucescens, also known as the hybrid of Quercus robur and Quercus petraea, is a deciduous tree species native to Europe, particularly in the United Kingdom. Its bark and leaves are rich in tannins and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for various medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Quercus × subglaucescens include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Quercus × subglaucescens is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its bark is also used to make a decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
